symptoms.symptom_name: Acute severe bloating with colicky abdominal pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Post-prandial gaseous distension and colic fit a functional/coeliac flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Still passing some wind; no bilious vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued passage of flatus and absence of bilious vomiting argue against complete mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colicky (not constant) pain, afebrile, no RLQ localisation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever, anorexia or RLQ localisation lowers appendicitis probability.
Pain proportionate to a young low-risk patient argues against ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Onset after large gluten meal with prior episodic gut symptoms; pending coeliac serology

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear gluten trigger with a pattern of prior episodes strongly supports a coeliac/functional flare.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Coeliac serology confirmation and gastroenterology/dietitian referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirm coeliac disease and establish a gluten-free diet electively via GP.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal imaging (erect AXR/CT) if red flags develop
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Only if obstruction/peritonism features appear — not indicated for a resolving functional flare.
